An open-source way to connect your AI to your files, tools, and apps.

Today we're sharing **Ganju** — an open-source platform that connects AI
assistants to the things you actually work with: your documents, your tools, and
your apps.

## Why we built it

AI assistants are great at reasoning but blind to your context. Wiring them up to
your data usually means running servers, writing glue code, and worrying about
security. Ganju removes all of that: create a connection, add your content and
tools, and your AI is ready — in minutes, no code required.

## What you can do

- Search and answer from your own files.
- Let your AI use real tools: email, calendar, web search, Slack, and more.
- Turn the same setup into a bot on Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, or Discord.

It's open source under Apache-2.0 — run it yourself, or let us host it for you.

[Get started free →](https://app.ganju.ai)

Get started free →
Create your first Ganju workspace and connect your AI in a few minutes.

Ganju connects your AI assistant to your own files, tools, and apps through a
single secure connection — no servers to run and no code to write.

## 1. Create a workspace

Sign up at [app.ganju.ai](https://app.ganju.ai) and create a project. This is
your private space where everything lives.

## 2. Add content and tools

- **Files** — upload documents or connect Google Drive / OneDrive.
- **Tools** — switch on email, calendar, web search, Slack, and more.
- **Prompts** — write instructions that shape how your AI behaves.

## 3. Connect your AI

Copy your connection URL and add it to Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor. Your AI can
now search your content and use your tools.

## 4. Share it

Turn the same setup into a bot on Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, or Discord so your
whole team can use it.

> Tip: every page on this site is available as Markdown — just add `.md` to the
> URL (you're reading [`/docs/getting-started.md`](/docs/getting-started.md)).

Ganju connects your AI assistant to your own files, tools, and apps through a single secure connection — no servers to run and no code to write.

1. Create a workspace

Sign up at app.ganju.ai and create a project. This is your private space where everything lives.

2. Add content and tools

  • Files — upload documents or connect Google Drive / OneDrive.
  • Tools — switch on email, calendar, web search, Slack, and more.
  • Prompts — write instructions that shape how your AI behaves.

3. Connect your AI

Copy your connection URL and add it to Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor. Your AI can now search your content and use your tools.

4. Share it

Turn the same setup into a bot on Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, or Discord so your whole team can use it.

Tip: every page on this site is available as Markdown — just add .md to the URL (you’re reading /docs/getting-started.md).
Link Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client to your workspace.

Ganju speaks the **Model Context Protocol (MCP)**, the standard way AI clients
connect to outside tools and information. Any MCP-compatible client can connect.

## Your connection URL

Each workspace has its own URL that looks like:

```
https://app.ganju.ai/mcp/your-workspace
```

Keep it private — it grants access to everything you've connected.

## Add it to a client

- **Claude** — add the URL as a custom MCP connector.
- **Cursor** — add it under MCP servers in settings.
- **ChatGPT / Gemini** — connect using your own account where supported.

Once connected, ask your assistant a question about your files or tell it to use
a tool — it will route through Ganju securely.
